data.title: Boeing set to hire replacements for striking factory workers - Bloomberg
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) plans to start hiring permanent replacements for some of the 3,200 hourly workers on strike at its St. Louis defense manufacturing plant, Bloomberg reported Thursday, with no resolution on the horizon for the month-long labor dispute.

Boeing (NYSE:BA) will begin posting openings for some of the positions held by striking union members, and hold a job fair on September 16, Bloomberg reported, citing an internal memo from Dan Gillian, a Boeing VP and the senior executive in St. Louis.

Members of the International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers District 837, who walked off the job a month ago, have twice rejected Boeing's (BA) offer, which Gillian described as the richest ever presented to workers at its defense factories in Missouri and Illinois.

Boeing (BA) is willing to make "minor adjustments" to its offer, which would raise wages for most workers by ~40% over four years, but union officials have demanded "more across the board, and that isn't part of how this goes forward," Gillian said, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: AI startup Sierra, led by former Salesforce exec, raises $350M at $10B valuation
data.description:
• Artificial intelligence startup Sierra said on Thursday that it had raised $350M in new funding, giving the company a $10B valuation.
  • The new funding round is being led by venture capital firm Greenoaks, Sierra said in a blog post. Greenoaks has previously invested in the company.
  • Sierra, which creates AI agents for customer experience teams and developers via its Agent OS, is led by former Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) co-CEO Bret Taylor. Taylor is also the Chairman of OpenAI, a board member at Shopify (SHOP) and has worked at Meta Platforms (META) and other tech companies.
  • “We will use our new funding – along with our strong revenue growth and balance sheet – to increase our investments in our platform, US growth, and international expansion,” Taylor wrote in the post.
  • Taylor continued: “We're in this for the long term – to create the platform on which the best customer experiences in the world are built. Making that dream a reality is all about execution: we need to deliver for every customer, every time, without exception. The opportunity ahead is enormous, for our customers, their customers, and Sierra.”

data.title: America's Car-Mart outlines inventory financing expansion and cost savings targets as tariff pressures ease
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: America's Car-Mart (CRMT) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Douglas Campbell reported that gross margin expanded to 36.6%, interest income increased 7.5%, and total collections rose by 6.2%, noting, "we stay disciplined on volume to protect returns and affordability." Campbell highlighted that demand remains solid with credit applications up 10% year-over-year and a higher conversion rate from website traffic. He explained, "we paced volume as tariffs and wholesale pricing created temporary constraints. We saw a knock-on effect from tariffs which drove a $500 per unit increase in the procurement cost during the quarter. This is incremental to the $300 I called out last quarter, but the increases we are seeing have since smoothed out."
  • Campbell emphasized the rollout of LOS V2, stating, "the new scorecard is delivering exactly what we designed it to do, shifting mix towards our highest ranked customers and away from lowest tiers." He also noted a shift, with "15% more of our volume came from ranks 5 through 7, while bookings in some of our lowest ranks were reduced by nearly 50%."
  • Chief Operating Officer Jamie Fischer said, "Total revenue for the quarter was $341.3 million, a decrease of 1.9% from the prior year, primarily resulting from fewer retail units sold." Fischer attributed lower volume to higher procurement costs and a strategic focus on higher-quality vehicles.
  • CFO Jonathan Collins added, "Operating expenses for SG&A totaled $51.4 million, a 10.1% increase from $46.7 million in the prior year. Roughly 2/3 of this increase was related to payroll growth...and 1/3 was driven by technology investments such as the rollout of LOS V2 and Pay Your Way." Collins stated, "we expect to unwind approximately half of total SG&A growth in the back half of the year."
  • Campbell summarized, "LOS V2 and the new scorecard are doing the work we intended, shifting mix towards our highest ranked customers under better pricing structures powered by risk-based pricing. And Pay Your Way is an upgrade that's laying the groundwork for more consistent payment behavior, operational efficiency and a lower cost of capital."

Outlook

  • Management stated they expect to unwind about half of the SG&A growth in the back half of the year and aim for "mid-16% SG&A as a percentage of retail sales" over time.
  • Campbell indicated that the focus remains on "quality, growth with affordability, serving more customers and protecting returns," and that payment and collections modernization will continue to be scaled.
  • The company is "actively evaluating actions to expand inventory capacity so it's not a limiting factor to sales going forward."

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for the quarter was $341.3 million, a decline of 1.9% year-over-year, primarily due to a 5.7% decrease in retail units sold to 13,568 units.
  • Interest income increased 7.5% and total collections rose 6.2% to $183.6 million, with the average collection per active customer rising to $585 from $562 last year.
  • Gross margin improved to 36.6%, a 160 basis point increase over the prior year quarter.
  • Operating expenses for SG&A were $51.4 million, up 10.1% from the prior year, with technology investments and payroll as main contributors. Management expects to reduce about half of this increase in the second half of the year.
  • Net charge-offs as a percentage of average finance receivables rose slightly to 6.6% from 6.4%. Delinquencies over 30 days were 3.8% at quarter end, up 30 basis points.
  • The allowance for credit losses improved to 23.35% from 25% last year, and the active customer account grew by 1.4% to almost 104,700 customers.
  • Interest expense decreased by 6.9% to $17 million, supported by improvements in the securitization platform. Recent securitization included a $172 million issuance at a weighted average coupon of 5.46%.

Q&A

  • Kyle Joseph, Stephens Inc.: Asked about procurement costs and volume trends post-quarter. Campbell responded, "we've seen the pricing smooth out. It's been sort of in that same exact range. In fact, it's come down a couple of bucks, but that's nominal...we've seen the same sort of demand we saw in July sort of flow through August."
  • Joseph asked about credit trends and expectations for stabilization. Campbell stated, "the portfolio is weighted with mostly this new underwriting in place...I think we should expect sort of the normal seasonal fluctuations within [NCOs]. And certainly, where we're at today is well within our operating range."
  • Joseph questioned SG&A cadence. Collins: "in the second half, we'll see roughly half of the increase from this quarter unwind...we should start seeing that benefit in the next fiscal year."
  • John Hecht, Jefferies LLC: Asked about the duration of tariff impacts on inventory pricing. Campbell said, "I would expect that through the balance of the year now that the effects of tariffs are sort of known that we get some seasonality and pricing decline in the back half, all other things being equal."
  • Hecht inquired about supply constraints and potential for recovery. Campbell noted, "with the release of LOS V2...I would expect us to continue to sort of continue to get favorability...it's really important given that we're seeing more growth at the top of the funnel and equal growth at the bottom, but more growth with these better qualified customers."
  • Additional web questions covered the impact of LOS V2 on rate structures and consumer health. Campbell explained that higher-rated customers received down payment and rate breaks, while lower-ranked customers faced higher down payments and rates. Fischer stated, "I think our customer base is always in a spot of being challenged with what's happening in the macro environment...although they are probably more constrained today than they were a year ago, our structures with the rollout of LOS, our structures are much better today than they were a year ago."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts asked about procurement costs, credit stabilization, SG&A trends, tariff impacts, and supply constraints, signaling a neutral to slightly cautious tone and pressing for clarity on operational headwinds and cost controls.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, frequently highlighting improvements in credit mix, gross margin expansion, and operational upgrades. In Q&A, responses remained measured, offering detail and reassurance about stabilization and forward initiatives.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from emphasizing operational turnaround and new leadership to a focus on executing enhancements and managing cost headwinds, while analysts' questions remained focused on risk and operational bottlenecks.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to a more concrete timeline for cost reductions, with management now stating they expect to unwind half of SG&A growth in the back half of the year, versus the prior quarter's broader statements on cost efficiency.
  • Strategic focus evolved from implementing foundational technology (Pay Your Way, risk-based pricing) to optimizing these systems and addressing inventory financing constraints.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on procurement costs, consumer health, and credit metrics, but current questions placed greater emphasis on the sustainability of cost controls and the operational impact of tariffs.
  • Key metric changes include a decrease in revenue and units sold, improved gross margin, and a slight uptick in credit losses and delinquencies compared to last year.
  • Management confidence remains high, with increased detail on tactical execution and a more forward-looking approach to unlocking inventory growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges cited include temporary constraints from tariffs and wholesale pricing, which increased procurement costs by $500 per unit, and ongoing pressure on inventory capacity under the current capital facility.
  • Management is "actively exploring alternative financing solutions to address these constraints and unlock additional capacity to serve our qualified customer demand."
  • Delinquencies increased by 30 basis points and net charge-offs ticked up, with management attributing this partly to legacy pools and the rollout of new payment technology causing temporary timing delays.
  • SG&A growth was identified as a concern, but management expects to unwind half of the increase in the second half of the year.

Final Takeaway

America's Car-Mart emphasized disciplined execution amid procurement cost pressures, with improvements in gross margin, collections, and portfolio quality. Management is focused on unlocking inventory growth through alternative financing, scaling technology-driven operational efficiencies, and expects to deliver meaningful SG&A cost reductions in the back half of the year, positioning the company for both near-term performance and long-term expansion as tariff headwinds subside and digital initiatives take hold.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tech Voices: Nvidia-Quantinuum, Fed AI job survey, OpenAI upskilling
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of announcements, statements and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

  • Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) venture capital arm is now a key investor in quantum computing company Quantinuum.

NVentures participated in Quantinuum's recent $600 million funding round, which valued the company at around $10 billion. 

"This capital raise will support Quantinuum’s advancement of quantum computing at scale, which includes continued progress toward the upcoming launch of Helios, the company’s next generation quantum computing system that is expected to launch this year," Quantiuum said in a statement. "It will also support Quantinuum’s path to becoming the first to perform universal fault-tolerant computing."

Honeywell (NASDAQ:HON) holds a majority stake in Quantinuum, which has reportedly been eyeing an IPO.

  • OpenAI, which is backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), said it's launching an AI certification program along with a platform to help businesses find workers with AI skills. 

“We believe fundamentally that AI will unlock more opportunities for more people than any technology in history, but it will also be disruptive,” said OpenAI CEO of Applications Fidji Simo, according to Bloomberg. “While we can’t eliminate the disruption, we can certainly help more people become fluent in AI and connect them with companies that need their skills.”

OpenAI made the announcement in conjunction with a White House summit on AI that included OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, Bloomberg added. The ChatGPT developer was co-founded by Tesla (TSLA) CEO Elon Musk. 

  • Meanwhile, economists at the Federal Reserve Bank of New York said a recent regional business survey indicated that AI doesn't appear to be significantly impacting hiring or firing, at least at this point.

"Businesses reported a notable increase in AI use over the past year, yet very few firms reported AI-induced layoffs. Indeed, for those already employed, our results indicate AI is more likely to result in retraining than job loss," the economists said in a blog post on Thursday.

"That said, AI is influencing recruiting, with some firms scaling back hiring due to AI and some firms adding workers proficient in its use. Looking ahead, however, layoffs and reductions in hiring plans due to AI use are expected to increase, especially for workers with a college degree," they added.

data.title: Gilead joins U.S. efforts in expanding access to new HIV drug in low-income nations
data.description:
• Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) said on Thursday that it has joined hands with the U.S. government to accelerate access to lenacapavir, its newly approved HIV PrEP therapy, for people living mainly in low- and lower-middle-income countries.
  • The company added that its three-year collaboration with the State Department and President’s Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR) builds on another tie-up the company signed in July with Swiss non-profit The Global Fund for the twice-yearly injectable.
  • Backed by the latest collaboration and its partnership with The Global Fund, the company is expected to supply lenacapavir for up to two million people living in the targeted regions at no profit.
  • In June, the FDA approved the HIV-1 capsid inhibitor for the prevention of HIV in pre-exposure prophylaxis ((PrEP)) and a month later, the European Commission also granted marketing authorization for the antiviral.

data.title: Roche gets EU CE mark for its Port Delivery platform containing Susvimo
data.description:
• Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) (OTCQX:RHHBF) said on Thursday that it has received the EU CE mark for its Port Delivery platform, which includes Susvimo, now called Contivue in Europe. 
  • Susvimo, a ranibizumab injection at a concentration of 100 mg/mL, is currently being reviewed by the European Medicines Agency for treating neovascular age-related macular degeneration.

data.title: VinFast Auto reports Q2 results
data.description:
• VinFast Auto press release (NASDAQ:VFS): Q2 total revenues were VND16,609.3 billion (US$663.0 million), an increase of 91.6% from the second quarter of 2024 and 1.9% higher than the first quarter of 2025.
  • Net loss was VND20,341.6 billion (US$812.0 million)

  • VinFast's EV deliveries were 35,837 in the second quarter of 2025, holding steady quarter-over-quarter and representing a 172% increase year-over-year.

    Cumulatively, in the first half of 2025, the Company delivered 72,167 EVs to customers globally, a 223% increase year-over-year.

    E-scooter and e-bike deliveries were 69,580 in the second quarter of 2025, a 55% increase quarter-over-quarter and a 432% increase year-over-year.

    Cumulatively, in the first half of 2025, the Company delivered 114,484 e-scooters and e-bikes, a 447% increase year-over-year.

    As of June 30, 2025, the Company had 394 showrooms globally.

  • The Company reaffirms its target to at least double global deliveries in 2025 and will continue to closely monitor the evolving macro-economic environment.

data.title: PENN Entertainment invests in the expansion of its key property in the Las Vegas Valley
data.description:
PENN Entertainment (NASDAQ:PENN) is investing in a major expansion of one of its key properties.

The M Resort Spa Casino in Las Vegas is undergoing a major, multi-million-dollar expansion, which is scheduled for completion in early 2026. The project includes a second hotel tower, which will nearly double guestroom capacity from 390 to 774 rooms and suites, and introduces 15,000 square feet of additional convention space and a new ballroom set to open in winter 2025. 

The first phase of the M Resort expansion featured a fully renovated and expanded 92,000-square-foot casino floor with upgraded table games, a refreshed high-limit room, modern lighting, new pathways, and enhanced guest seating. The company highlighted that design enhancements emphasize the desert’s natural beauty and aim to elevate the resort's identity as a luxury retreat. Other improvements include new restaurant concepts, upgraded amenities, and expanded partnerships, notably leveraging its role as the official team hotel for the NFL’s Las Vegas Raiders, whose training facility is nearby. 

The M Resort Spa Casino is located in Henderson, Nevada, about eight miles south of the Las Vegas Strip, and marks the southernmost casino in the Las Vegas Valley. 

The M Resort is owned by Gaming and Leisure Properties (NASDAQ:GLPI) and operated by PENN Entertainment (NASDAQ:PENN), which is responsible for its day-to-day management and ongoing expansion projects.

Other significant PENN (NASDAQ:PENN) properties include Hollywood Casino at PENN National Race Course in Pennsylvania, Hollywood Casino at Charles Town Races in West Virginia, Hollywood Casino at Greektown in downtown Detroit, Hollywood Casino at Kansas Speedway in Kansas City, and Hollywood Casino Joliet in Illinois, which opened at a new location in August.

Shares of PENN Entertainment (PENN) have perked up over the last six weeks, with a 12% rally. Notably, short interest on PENN stands at 13.6% of the total float.

data.title: Expect a disappointing jobs report Friday, healthcare job trends show warning signs – Aura Intelligence
data.description:
Evan Sohn, CEO of global workforce data and analytics company Aura Intelligence, expects tomorrow’s jobs report to disappoint based on concerning trends in job postings data.

“We saw a falloff in July. We saw a bigger fall off in August. So, we certainly expect there to be a disappointing [report],” Sohn said in an interview with CNBC.

Sohn’s company analyzes job posting data, which serves as a leading indicator for actual hiring that typically occurs the following month. This forward-looking perspective provides insights into employment trends before they appear in official statistics.

“We’re looking at the jobs that are being posted now, which would be filled next month,” Sohn explained.

In addition, he said the healthcare sector (NYSEARCA:XLV) is flashing particularly worrisome signals. Healthcare job postings are down 9% month-over-month and have declined 32% for the year. This represents a significant shift for what has traditionally been considered a recession-proof sector.

“This was a recession proof sector, and this is really, this is really scary, I think,” Sohn said. “When you have one out of every eight people in the U.S. hired in the healthcare sector, and it was recession proof, if it’s going to see a decline, that’s really going to take its toll on the overall job economy.”

The data also points to AI’s growing impact on workforce dynamics. While most sectors show declining job postings, computer software stands alone with positive growth – 2% year-over-year and 4% month-over-month for August. However, Sohn questions whether this represents genuine job creation or simply a shift toward developing AI that will ultimately replace other workers.

“AI is certainly going to take jobs from people that don’t know AI,” Sohn stated firmly. He referenced Salesforce CEO Mark Benioff’s recent comments about replacing laid-off employees with AI support services as evidence of this ongoing transition. “If you have AI that do a great job coding, I need fewer people coding. I can make fewer investments into coding because I have fewer people doing that.”

More on the U.S. economy:

  • ISM Services Index rises more than expected in August on new orders
  • U.S. Jobs Data Key To Greenback's Near-Term Fate
  • Stagflation Express Is Gaining Speed
  • Initial jobless claims rise slightly more than expected in past week
  • Vanguard sees a sweet spot for bond investors as yields remain elevated

data.title: Public Firms' Bitcoin holdings surpass 1 million BTC
data.description:
• Publicly traded companies now hold a combined total of 1,000,632 bitcoins (BTC-USD), valued at around $110 billion, according to BTC Treasuries data.
  • Strategy (MSTR) is the largest corporate holder, accounting for nearly 63.6% of the total with 636,505 BTC, while the top 10 companies combined hold 863,397 BTC, nearly 86%.
  • Corporate bitcoin accumulation began in earnest with Strategy in 2020, followed by Metaplanet (OTC:MTPLF) in 2024, with notable growth continuing into 2025.
  • Other significant holders include MARA Holdings (MARA) having 50,639 bitcoins, Bullish (BLSH) 24,000 bitcoins, and RIOT Platforms (RIOT) holding 19,239 bitcoins. 
  • Bitcoin dominance eased to just above 58% and bitcoin retreated to below $110,000 after it hit a record high above $124,000.
  • Strategy shares have fallen roughly 30% since their mid-July 2025 peak, while Metaplanet has lost about two-thirds of its value since late May.
  • Source
